title.en: God of Celestial Fire and Smithcraft

summary.zh: 编年史插注把希腊锻神赫菲斯托斯译作"斯瓦罗格"，称太阳神达日博格为其子；"斯瓦罗日奇"则指受供奉的火。
summary.en: A chronicle gloss renders Hephaestus as Svarog and makes the sun god Dazhbog his son; "Svarozhich" named the sacred fire itself.

description.zh:
斯瓦罗格是天火与锻造之神。他的主要出典是《伊帕季耶夫编年史》1114年条：编年史家在迻译拜占庭作家约翰·马拉拉斯的编年史时加了插注，把希腊锻造之神赫菲斯托斯译作"斯瓦罗格"，并称太阳神达日博格是"斯瓦罗格之子"。这段文字说，斯瓦罗格为王的时代铁钳自天而降，人们从此学会锻造兵器，他还立下一夫一妻的法度，违者投入火炉。

"斯瓦罗日奇"（意为斯瓦罗格之子）在古罗斯的训诫文书里指被当作神来供奉的火——教士斥责农民"向火祈祷，称它斯瓦罗日奇"，地点常在烘干谷物的烘谷房。波罗的海南岸的西斯拉夫人拜一位名字对应的神祇：梅泽堡主教蒂特马尔在11世纪初记载，卢蒂奇人的圣城拉多加斯特（雷特拉）神庙中立着头戴盔甲的战神"祖阿拉西奇"（Zuarasici），部落出征前来此问卜。

直接史料只有寥寥数条，斯瓦罗格的谱系大半出于学者构拟；19世纪以来的浪漫主义著述常把他拔高为斯拉夫的"天父"与众神之祖，这在中世纪文献里找不到依据。词源上，他的名字常与印度-伊朗语的"svar"（天光、太阳）相比较，也有人主张源自斯拉夫语"svara"（争斗），至今没有定论。
description.en:
Svarog is the god of heavenly fire and the smith's craft. His chief attestation is the entry for 1114 in the Hypatian Codex, where the chronicler, translating the Byzantine chronicle of John Malalas, added a gloss rendering the Greek smith god Hephaestus as Svarog and calling the sun god Dazhbog "son of Svarog." In this passage, tongs fell from the sky during Svarog's reign and people learned to forge weapons; he also instituted the law of one husband and one wife, casting offenders into a furnace.

In Old Rus sermons "Svarozhich," son of Svarog, named the fire that peasants venerated as a god: churchmen complained that people "pray to fire, calling it Svarozhich," typically at the drying barn where grain was cured. The West Slavs on the southern Baltic shore worshipped a deity of the corresponding name: Thietmar of Merseburg recorded in the early eleventh century that the temple at Radogost (Rethra), holy city of the Lutici, housed an armoured war god called Zuarasici, consulted by lot before campaigns.

With so few direct sources, most of Svarog's genealogy is scholarly reconstruction; Romantic writers from the nineteenth century onward inflated him into a Slavic sky-father and progenitor of the gods, for which the medieval texts give no warrant. Etymologists compare his name with Indo-Iranian svar, "sunlight," while others derive it from Slavic svara, "quarrel"; the question stays open.

value.zh: 赫菲斯托斯（编年史插注）
太阳神达日博格；"斯瓦罗日奇"即圣火
value.en: The sun god Dazhbog; "Svarozhich" the sacred fire

value.zh: 雷特拉神庙的祖阿拉西奇

sources.zh: 《伊帕季耶夫编年史》1114年条（约翰·马拉拉斯编年史译文插注）
sources.en: Hypatian Codex, entry for 1114 (gloss on the chronicle of John Malalas)

sources.zh: 梅泽堡的蒂特马尔《编年史》（11世纪初）
sources.en: Thietmar of Merseburg, Chronicon, early 11th century
